one_time_description: Town of Comox revenue of $1,821,000 with no prior year comparative and no budget, likely a one-time payment; also Specific Claims revenue of $2,110,096 in prior year was non-recurring
economic_notes: Nation holds investments in multiple limited partnerships including fisheries (A-Tlegay Fisheries LP), forestry (Nanwakolas Timber LP, La-kwa sa muqw LP, TFL 64 LP), carbon credits (Nanwakolas Offset LP), and grizzly bear tourism (Knight Inlet Grizzly Lodge via Timber LP). GBEs had combined revenue of $15,376,512 and net income of $7,278,226 in 2024. Nation is also involved in treaty negotiations with BC and Canada. Comox Valley Project Watershed Society made improvements to jointly-owned land with City of Courtenay totalling $657,724 (Nation's 51% share).
notes: Nation accounts for 7 GBE partnerships using modified equity method rather than consolidation; GBE total assets were $164,974,214 which are not on Nation's balance sheet. Loans receivable of $22,037,668 includes significant amounts to K'ómoks First Nation Business Trust ($16,775,071) and KFN Economic Development LP ($4,655,838) with no specific repayment terms. Province of BC revenue jumped significantly from $2,165,871 to $8,738,053 - large increase likely related to specific grants or settlements. Town of Comox $1,821,000 revenue appears non-recurring with no prior year amount or budget. No schedule of remuneration provided. Annual debt service on CMHC loan scheduled at $19,292 for 2025 (first year of new loan). Total government transfers calculated as ISC $3,141,164 + Province of BC $8,738,053 + BC Gaming LP $415,414 + First Nations Health Authority $723,592 + BC Treaty Commission $1,030,000 + Comox Valley Regional District $5,000 = $14,053,223 plus Federal Government deferred revenue recognized and other federal items; OSR estimated as total revenue less identified government transfers.
